title: Production of recombinant interferon inhibitor B18R
author: Alexandr V. Shustov
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kp8cvrw

steps.title: Prepare a night culture by inoculating...
steps.instruction: Prepare a night culture by inoculating of 5 ml of Luria-Bertani (LB) medium with kanamycin (50 ug/ml) with 5 ul of a cryo-culture of an expression strain. The expression strain is BL21(DE3) transformed with a plasmid pET28/B18R(HisTag). Incubate overnight at 37°C on a shaker (150 rpm).

steps.title: Next day transfer the night culture...
steps.instruction: Next day transfer the night culture to 1L of Luria-Bertani (LB) medium with kanamycin (50 ug/ml). Incubate at 37°C on a shaker (150 rpm) until an optical density at 600 nm is 0.8. At this point add isopropyl β-D-thiogalactoside (IPTG) to 1 mM. The induced culture should be grown for 6 hrs at 37°C (150 rpm).

steps.title: Collect bacterial cells by centrifugation (8000 rpm, 10 min)
steps.instruction: Collect bacterial cells by centrifugation (8000 rpm, 10 min). Wash the bacterial biomass with water. For this purpose resuspend the pellet in 50 ml of water and pellet the bacterial cells by centrifugation (8000 rpm, 10 min).

steps.title: Resuspend the bacterial biomass to obtain...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the bacterial biomass to obtain a 10% suspension in a lysis buffer (20% sucrose, 20 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 5 mM MgCl 2 , 0.1% Triton-X100). Add 1/100 of the suspension volume of a lysozyme stock, 1/100 volume of DNAse I stock, 1/100 volume of RNase stock, 1/1000 volume of PMSF stock. Incubate the mixture at room temperature for 1 hr.

steps.title: Add to the mixture a dry...
steps.instruction: Add to the mixture a dry powder of sodium deoxycholate (SDC) to 0.5% (w/v). Dissolve SDC.

steps.title: Treat the mixture on a ultrasonic disruptor
steps.instruction: Treat the mixture on a ultrasonic disruptor. Make 20 pulses at 50% amplitude, each pulse 30 sec, with 3 min pauses, on ice bath. Make sure that a size of a sonotrode matches a volume of the mixture. The mixture should become not viscous.

steps.title: Separate by centrifugation an insoluble fraction which contains inclusion bodies
steps.instruction: Separate by centrifugation an insoluble fraction which contains inclusion bodies. For this purpose centrifuge the mixture (12000 rpm, 30 min, 4°C).

steps.title: To remove bacterial lipids the pellet...
steps.instruction: To remove bacterial lipids the pellet has to be washed once in a wash buffer with SDC (100 mM Tris pH 8.0, 2 mM EDTA, 0.5% SDC). For this purpose, resuspend the inclusion bodies to make a 10% suspension in the wash buffer with SDC. Sonicate the suspension briefly to disintegrate chunks of the pellet. Pellet the inclusion bodies by centrifugation as described. Wash the inclusion bodies in a buffer TE (100 mM Tris pH 8.0, 2 mM EDTA). Resuspend the inclusion bodies (to 10%) in TE without SDC, sonicate briefly and re-pellet. Repeat the wash in TE one more time.

steps.title: Weight the pellet of the washed inclusion bodies
steps.instruction: Weight the pellet of the washed inclusion bodies. Divide the pellet into portions by 70 mg. Store the inclusion bodies at -80°C before further processing. (Note: typical yields of the inclusion bodies are 700 mg from 1 L of an induced culture)

steps.title: Dissolve a portion (70 mg) of...
steps.instruction: Dissolve a portion (70 mg) of the inclusion bodies in 100 ml of a solubilization buffer (50 mM Tris pH 10, 2% sodium lauroyl sarcosinate (SLS)). Stir the suspension at room temperature for 2 hours

steps.title: Add CuSO 4 to the protein...
steps.instruction: Add CuSO 4 to the protein solution to a final concentration 50 µM. Stir the protein solution in an open flask on a magnetic stirrer for 20 hrs at room temperature

steps.title: Precipitate the protein from the solution
steps.instruction: Precipitate the protein from the solution. For this purpose, add 25 g of ammonium sulfate ((NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 ) to 100 ml of the protein solution (final concentration of ammonium sulfate 40% w/v). Incubate the resulting solution for 2 hrs in ice bath. Precipitate the protein by centrifugation (10000 rpm, 20 min)

steps.title: Dissolve the precipitate in 20 ml...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the precipitate in 20 ml of a buffer A for metal-affinity chromatography (IMAC) containing 6M urea (20 mM Na 2 HPO 4 pH 7.4, 500 mM NaCl, 20 mM imidazole, 6M urea). Remove undissolved material was by centrifugation.

steps.title: Perform a metal-affinity chromatography
steps.instruction: Perform a metal-affinity chromatography. Use a column His GraviTrap (GE Healthcare) which can be prepared according to the manufacturer's instructions. Equilibrate the column by passing through it 10 ml of the buffer A+6M urea. Pass the protein solution (from the previous step) through the column. Wash the column with 20 ml of the buffer A+6M urea. Elute the protein by passing through the column of 10 ml of a buffer B for IMAC+6M urea (20 mM Na 2 HPO 4 pH 7.4, 500 mM NaCl, 500 mM imidazole, 6M urea). Collect the eluate.

steps.title: Subject the collected eluate to dialysis...
steps.instruction: Subject the collected eluate to dialysis against 2 L of PBS. Make dialysis for two days at +8°C, with three buffer changes during the dialysis. Typically no precipitation is observedduring the dialysis.

steps.title: Filter-sterilize the B18R solution through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter-sterilize the B18R solution through a 0.22-um filter and store at -80°C. The B18R solution must be clear. Measure the protein concentration using the Bradford method. Typical concentrations at this stage are >500 ug/ml.
